title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy recipe. Get creamy, flavorful results at home, perfect for lunch or dinner!
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, a classic Indian Murgh Makhani dish perfect for any meal.

description: Experience the rich, creamy indulgence of Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a beloved Indian classic. This dish features tender chicken simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter, cream, and aromatic spices. Perfect for a comforting and flavorful meal that truly captures the essence of Indian cuisine.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until lightly browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Saute for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Mixture
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, cumin powder, coriander powder, and a pinch of salt. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods. Carefully transfer the tomato mixture and the soaked cashews to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step is crucial for the signature smooth Butter Chicken texture.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Return the pan with the strained gravy to low heat. Stir in the cooked chicken, remaining 1 tablespoon of butter, crushed kasuri methi, and heavy cream. Add sugar if using, to balance the acidity. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors and the gravy to thicken slightly. Adjust salt if needed. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

tips: For best results, marinate the chicken overnight. The longer it marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be.
Do not skip blending and straining the gravy. This step is key to achieving the incredibly smooth, restaurant-style texture of Murgh Makhani.
Serve Butter Chicken with warm naan bread, rice, or roti to soak up all the delicious, creamy sauce.
article:
Mastering the Art of Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani): Your Ultimate Indian Lunch Recipe

Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Butter Chicken, known in India as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian lunch recipe has transcended borders, becoming a global favorite for its rich, creamy, and subtly sweet-spicy profile. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that restaurant-quality experience in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to prepare an authentic and utterly delicious Butter Chicken that will impress even the most discerning connoisseurs.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken is a quintessential Indian dish originating from Delhi in the 1950s. It was reportedly created by Kundan Lal Jaggi and Kundan Lal Gujral at the Moti Mahal restaurant. The name "Murgh Makhani" literally translates to "chicken with butter," aptly describing its core components. At its heart, it features succulent pieces of chicken (often tandoor-cooked or pan-fried) swimming in a luscious, vibrant orange-red gravy. This gravy is a marvel of flavors, typically made from tomatoes, cashews, cream, and a blend of aromatic spices. The defining characteristic is its velvety texture and a delicate balance of tanginess from the tomatoes, sweetness from a touch of sugar or honey, and a mild warmth from spices like garam masala and fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). It’s the ultimate comfort food, perfect for a hearty Indian lunch or a special dinner.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken

To make this exquisite Butter Chicken, quality ingredients are key. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri for color)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Makhani (Butter) Gravy:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter
*   1 tbsp oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp cumin powder
*   4-5 medium tomatoes, roughly chopped (or 1 can 400g crushed tomatoes)
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, optional but recommended for creaminess)
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1 tsp sugar or honey (adjust to taste)
*   1/2 tsp kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   Salt to taste

For Finishing:
*   1/4 cup heavy cream
*   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Recipe Instructions

Follow these steps for a truly memorable Murgh Makhani:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Mix well, ensuring all chicken pieces are coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook in batches if necessary. Sear the chicken for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly charred and cooked through. Remove the chicken and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, add 2 tbsp butter and remaining 1 tbsp oil. Once the butter melts, add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Add Aromatics and Spices:** Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and cumin powder. Sauté for 30 seconds, stirring continuously to prevent burning.
5.  **Build the Gravy:** Add the chopped tomatoes and soaked cashews (if using). Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes soften and release their juices. Add 1/2 cup water or broth, bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for another 5-7 minutes until everything is very soft.
6.  **Blend the Gravy:** Remove the pan from heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender or use an immersion blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.
7.  **Simmer and Finish:** Return the smooth gravy to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces, sugar or honey, and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich gravy.
8.  **The Creamy Touch:** Stir in the heavy cream. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, but do not boil after adding the cream, as it can curdle. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed.
9.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

For truly exceptional Butter Chicken, consider these expert tips:

*   **Marination Time is Key:** The longer you marinate the chicken, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination is ideal.
*   **Char the Chicken:** Don't skip searing the chicken. The slightly charred edges add a wonderful smoky depth that is characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani, mimicking a tandoor oven.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** A perfectly smooth gravy is crucial. If your blender isn't powerful enough, straining the gravy after blending will remove any remaining bits of onion or tomato skin, resulting in a luxurious texture.
*   **Kashmiri Red Chili Powder:** Use Kashmiri red chili powder for its vibrant color and mild heat. If you only have regular chili powder, use less or mix with paprika for color.
*   **Don't Skimp on Butter:** It's called Butter Chicken for a reason! Good quality unsalted butter contributes significantly to the richness and flavor profile.
*   **Kasoori Methi is a Must:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their aromatic bitterness, which balances the sweetness of the gravy.
*   **Adjust Sweetness and Spice:** The balance of sweet, tangy, and spicy is personal. Adjust the sugar/honey and chili powder to your preference.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Enjoy your homemade Butter Chicken with a variety of accompaniments to complete the Indian lunch experience:

*   **Naan Bread:** Warm, fluffy naan (plain, garlic, or butter naan) is the quintessential pairing, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or a fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast and helps absorb the creamy sauce.
*   **Raita:** A cooling cucumber or mint raita (yogurt dip) helps balance the richness of the dish.
*   **Onion Salad:** A simple salad of sliced red onions, a squeeze of lemon juice, and a sprinkle of chaat masala adds a refreshing crunch.
*   **Pickles:** A small side of Indian mixed pickle can add a zesty kick.

## Variations and Substitutions

If you're looking to modify your Butter Chicken, here are a few ideas:

*   **Healthier Option:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute with low-fat cream or coconut milk for a dairy-free version. You can also grill or bake the chicken instead of pan-frying.
*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and potatoes for a delicious vegetarian Murgh Makhani.
*   **Spicier Version:** Increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the gravy base.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an extra smoky touch, you can use the "dhungar" method: place a small bowl with a hot piece of charcoal in the gravy, drizzle a little ghee over it, and cover for a few minutes.
*   **No Cashews:** If you have a nut allergy or don't have cashews, you can substitute with blanched almonds or skip them entirely, though the gravy will be slightly less rich.

Mastering the art of Butter Chicken is a rewarding culinary journey. With this detailed recipe and helpful tips, you're well on your way to creating a dish that embodies the warmth, comfort, and exquisite flavors of Indian cuisine. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to delight your senses with this beloved Murgh Makhani for your next unforgettable Indian lunch.
